comment.body: two things to take into consideration here: Alma's analysis is based on the mm positioning of yesterday evening. So depending on customer flow during the day the profile can and will change. Most of the time not enough to alter the main characteristics, but sometimes it can. Today could be such a day. But I have no indication either way, just guessing. Second is as you mentioned customer flow in spot, which can suppress or accelerate the effects discussed

comment.body: Any transaction is per se gamma neutral. The buyer is long, the seller short in equal amounts. But the MM hedges their exposure while the other side often doesn't or not as tightly. Therefore this creates predictable hedging activity that has an effect on the market. So you need to weight your numbers by the notional amount of options bought/sold by the MM
comment.raw_body: Any transaction is per se gamma neutral. The buyer is long, the seller short in equal amounts. But the MM hedges their exposure while the other side often doesn't or not as tightly. Therefore this creates predictable hedging activity that has an effect on the market. So you need to weight your numbers by the notional amount of options bought/sold by the MM
